studyName: Youth Drug Abuse, ADHD and Related Disorders

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, affection status of the subject for ADHD or drug abuse, sex, race, age, disorders of alcohol, marijuana, or tobacco use, scores of six candidate genes (COMY, DRD2, DRD4, MAOA, SLC6A3/DAT1, and SLC6A4/5-HTTLPR), and ten principal components of ethnic ancestry.

variables.name: @5HTTLPR
variables.description: Original 5-HTTLPR candidate gene score (long vs. short risk allele)

variables.name: @5HTTLPR_add
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for 5-HTTLPR candidate gene,  0-1-2, 2 indicates the highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: AGE1stTOB
variables.description: Age first used tobacco

variables.name: ALCHIWATERAGE29
variables.description: Age of hiwater symptoms PRIOR TO AGE 30 (youngest age if hiwater occurred multiple times)

variables.name: ALCHIWATERSYMCOU29
variables.description: Highest number of endorsed symptoms for alcohol use disorder PRIOR TO AGE 30

variables.name: Affection_Status
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for ADHD or drug abuse

variables.name: COMT
variables.description: Original COMT snp score

variables.name: COMT_add
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for COMT snp,  0-1-2, 2 indicates the highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: DAT1
variables.description: Original DRD4 candidate gene score

variables.name: DAT1_2
variables.description: Original DAT1 candidate gene score recoded into long vs. short risk risk allele

variables.name: DAT1_add
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for DAT1 candidate gene, 0-1-2, 2 indicates the highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: DRD2
variables.description: Original DRD2 snp score

variables.name: DRD2_add
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for DRD2 snp, 0-1-2, 2 indicates the highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: DRD4
variables.description: Original DRD4 candidate gene score

variables.name: DRD4_2
variables.description: Original DRD4 candidate gene score recodedinto long vs. short risk risk allele

variables.name: DRD4_add
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for DRD4 candidate gene, 0-1-2, 2 indicates highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: Gen_Risk
variables.description: Summed score of the 6 candidate genes (MAOA_add2, DRD4_add, DATA1_add, @5HTTLPR_add, COMT_add, DRD2_add)

variables.name: MAOA
variables.description: Original MAOA candidate gene score

variables.name: MAOA_2
variables.description: Original MAOA candidate gene score recoded into long vs. short risk risk allele

variables.name: MAOA_add
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for MAOA candidate gene 0-1-2, 2 indicates highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: MAOA_add2
variables.description: Additive coding of risk allele for MAOA candidate gene, 0-2, excluding heterozygous females, 2 indicates highest risk, 0 lowest risk

variables.name: MJHIWATERAGE29
variables.description: Age of hiwater symptoms PRIOR TO AGE 30 (youngest age if hiwater occurred multiple times)

variables.name: MJHIWATERSYMCOU29
variables.description: Highest number of endorsed symptoms for marijuana use disorder PRIOR TO AGE 30

variables.name: PC1
variables.description: First of ten principal components of ethnic ancestry

variables.description: Tenth of ten principal components of ethnic ancestry

variables.description: Second of ten principal components of ethnic ancestry

variables.description: Seventh of ten principal components of ethnic ancestry

variables.description: Eighth of ten principal components of ethnic ancestry

variables.name: PC9

variables.name: PEIDEVIANTBEHAV
variables.description: Pei-i scale Deviant Behavior.  Past year.

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: TOBSYMCOU
variables.description: Symptom count for tobacco use disorder IN PAST 12 MONTHS

variables.name: TOBSYMCOUprior
variables.description: Symptom count for lifetime tobacco use disorder prior to past year

variables.name: TOBUSE
variables.description: Have you ever used tobacco?

variables.name: age
variables.description: Subject age

variables.name: alc_evuse
variables.description: Alcohol ever used

variables.name: ethnic
variables.description: Participant ethnicity inferred from genotypes

variables.name: gen_risk_missing
variables.description: The number of missing candidate gene scores, if missing more than 1, than missing on Gen_Risk

variables.name: mj_evuse
variables.description: Cannabis ever used

variables.description: Tenth of ten principal components of ethnic ancestry *100 (to use as covariates in candidate gene analyses)

variables.description: Participant self-reported race

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site, histological type, analyte type, and tumor status.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The table also includes sample use.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs and consent information.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
